docs.title: Welcome to Arachnid
docs.text: Arachnid is the ultimate infrastructure tool for your Discord server. It combines visual architecture design with high-fidelity music, global economy, and advanced moderation.

docs.title: Server Architect
docs.text: Design your server visually using the Wizard. Create categories, channels, and roles in an interactive menu, then build them instantly.

docs.title: Hi-Fi Audio
docs.text: Stream high-quality music from YouTube, SoundCloud, and Bandcamp with advanced audio filters.

docs.title: Global Economy
docs.text: A persistent economy system where your currency follows you across every server.

docs.title: Smart Moderation
docs.text: Automated security, timed bans, logging, and a robust verification system.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Get your server running in minutes.

Invite Arachnid Setup Guide

docs.title: The Architect Wizard
docs.text: The Architect Wizard is Arachnid's signature feature. It allows you to visually design and build your server's infrastructure.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Wizard is an interactive "Blueprint" editor. You define what you want your server to look like, and Arachnid builds it for you.

graph TD A[Start Wizard] --> B{Design Blueprint} B --> C[Add Categories] B --> D[Add Channels] B --> E[Define Roles] B --> F[Toggle Features] F --> G[Build Server] G --> H[Finished Infrastructure]

docs.title: Using the Wizard
docs.text:
Run the command to open the interface:

/wizard

docs.title: 1. The Blueprint Menu
docs.text:
You will see a menu with several buttons:

  • Add Category: Creates a folder (e.g., "Welcome", "Gaming").
  • Add Text: Adds a text channel to a specific category.
  • Add Voice: Adds a voice channel to a specific category.
  • Add Role: Creates a role with preset permissions.

docs.title: 2. Feature Toggles
docs.text:
Enable built-in systems to have Archanid automatically create their required channels:

  • Logs: Creates #mod-logs for audit trails.
  • Community: Creates #rules and #announcements.

docs.title: 3. Build Process
docs.text:
Once your blueprint is ready, click BUILD SERVER. Archanid will create the channels, sort them, and assign permissions automatically.

Note

The Wizard appends new channels to your server. It does not delete your existing channels.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Arachnid offers advanced configuration options for bot owners and server administrators. This section covers managing bot ownership, privileged users, and global settings.

docs.title: Bot Owners
docs.text:
Bot owners have absolute control over the bot, including access to the eval command and the ability to manage privileged users.

Security Warning

NEVER add a stranger as a bot owner. They can execute arbitrary code on your host machine.

docs.title: Managing Owners
docs.text:
Only the original application owner (the Discord account that created the bot) can add new owners initially.

  • Add Owner: 
    /settings add_owner user:@username
  • Remove Owner: 
    /settings remove_owner user:@username
  • List Owners: 
    /settings list_owners

docs.title: Privileged Users
docs.text: Privileged users are trusted members who are granted access to specific restricted features without being full bot owners. This is useful for giving a friend access to the music system's admin controls without giving them shell access.

docs.title: Granting Privileges
docs.text:
Use the /settings add_privileged_user command. You can assign multiple privileges by separating them with commas.

Syntax: 

/settings add_privileged_user user:@username privileges:music,gamble

docs.title: Available Privileges
docs.text: Privilege Description music Bypass music voting requirements, force skip, and manage the player. gamble Access restricted economy/gambling admin tools. media Access advanced image processing features that might be resource-heavy. lastfm Access restricted Last.fm management tools. all Full access to all restricted user commands.

docs.title: Managing Privileges
docs.text:
• Remove Privileges: 
    /settings remove_privileged_user user:@username
  • List Privileged Users: 
    /settings list_privileged_users

docs.title: Custom Prefix
docs.text:
While Arachnid is designed for Slash Commands (/), it also supports legacy text commands. You can change the prefix for your server.

Command: 

/prefix new_prefix:!
* Default: ! * Example: /prefix ? will change the bot to respond to ?help.

docs.title: Installation & Setup
docs.text: Setting up Arachnid is straightforward. Follow this guide to ensure the bot has the necessary permissions to function correctly.

docs.title: 1. Invite the Bot
docs.text:
Click the button below to invite Arachnid to your server.

Invite Arachnid

Critical Permissions

Arachnid acts as a Server Architect. This means it needs to modify channels, manage roles, and delete messages.

Ensure you grant the Administrator permission when inviting. Without this, the /wizard and Moderation features will fail.

docs.title: 2. Verify Presence
docs.text:
Once the bot joins your server:

  1. Check that the bot is online.
  2. Ensure it has a role higher than the members it needs to moderate.
  3. Type / in any channel to verify that Slash Commands are registered.

Don't see commands?

If typing / does not show Arachnid's commands:

  1. Go to Server Settings > Integrations > Bots & Apps.
  2. Ensure Arachnid is listed.
  3. If not, kick the bot and re-invite it using the link above.

docs.title: 3. Initial Configuration
docs.text:
Before opening your server to the public, run the Architect Wizard.

  • Command: /wizard
  • Goal: Create necessary channels (e.g., mod-logs, music, general).

Next: The Architect Wizard

docs.title: Setup Guide
docs.text: Welcome to the Arachnid setup guide. This page will walk you through the process of inviting the bot, configuring its initial permissions, and using the Server Architect to build your community in seconds.

docs.title: 1. Inviting the Bot
docs.text:
To get started, you need to invite Arachnid to your server.

Permissions

Arachnid requires Administrator permissions to function correctly as a "Server Architect". It needs to create channels, manage roles, and moderate users.

Click here to Invite Arachnid

  1. Select your server from the dropdown menu.
  2. Click Authorize.
  3. Complete the Captcha.

docs.title: 2. The Server Architect (First Run)
docs.text: Once Arachnid joins, the most powerful feature at your disposal is the Server Architect. This tool allows you to design your server's layout (Categories, Channels, Roles) visually and then builds it for you automatically.

docs.title: Running the Wizard
docs.text:
Type the following command in any channel:

/wizard

Requirement

You must be an Administrator to use this command.

docs.title: Using the Interface
docs.text:
The Wizard will open an interactive blueprint menu.

  1. Add Category: Click this to create a folder (e.g., "General", "Staff", "Gaming").
  2. Add Text/Voice Channel: Click these buttons to add channels to your categories. You will be asked which Category they belong to.
  3. Add Role: Define your server's hierarchy (e.g., "Owner", "Moderator", "Member").
  4. Toggle Features: Enable built-in systems like Leveling, Tickets, or Logging to have the bot automatically set up the necessary channels for them.

docs.title: Building
docs.text:
Once you are happy with your blueprint, click the green BUILD SERVER button.

Arachnid will now:

  • Create all the Roles you defined.
  • Create the Categories.
  • Create Text and Voice channels inside those categories.
  • Set up permissions automatically.

docs.title: 3. Post-Setup Checklist
docs.text:
After the Architect finishes, your server structure is ready. Here are the next recommended steps:

  • Set the Prefix: If you prefer text commands, set a custom prefix. 
    /prefix new_prefix:?
  • Configure Logs: If you enabled the Logging feature, ensure the mod-logs channel is private.
  • Test Music: Join a voice channel and try playing a song to ensure audio permissions are correct. 
    /music play query:lofi hip hop

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
The Wizard isn't creating channels?

Ensure Arachnid has the Administrator permission and that its role is higher than the roles it is trying to manage in the Server Settings > Roles list.

I can't see the slash commands?

If slash commands aren't appearing, try re-inviting the bot or checking your User Settings > Text & Images > "Use Slash Commands".

docs.title: Server Architect Module
docs.text: The Server Architect module transforms the process of setting up a server into a visual, interactive experience.

docs.title: The Blueprint System
docs.text: When you run /wizard, you are creating a temporary Blueprint. This allows you to experiment with your server's layout without making any immediate changes.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Categories: The folders that hold your channels.
  • Channels: Text and Voice channels assigned to specific categories.
  • Roles: The permission hierarchy (e.g., Owner, Admin, Member).

docs.title: Automated Setup
docs.text:
The Wizard can automatically configure the infrastructure for other Arachnid modules.

Feature Created Channels Purpose Community #rules, #announcements Standard information channels. Logs #mod-logs Private channel for moderation logs. Tickets (Configuration only) Prepares the database for ticket systems.

docs.title: Global Economy Module
docs.text: The Economy module is Global. This means your currency (Credits) and items are tied to your User ID, not the specific server. If you get rich in Server A, you can spend that money in Server B (if they have a shop enabled).

docs.title: Core Concepts
docs.text:
• Wallet: Cash on hand. Can be stolen.
  • Bank: Secure storage. Cannot be stolen.
  • Inventory: Items you own.
  • Net Worth: Total value of Wallet + Bank + Inventory.

docs.title: Earning Credits
docs.text: There are multiple loops to earn currency.

docs.title: 1. Active Income (Commands)
docs.text:
• /work - Work a shift. Wages increase with your "Experience" level.
    • Cooldown: 1 hour
  • /crime - Attempt a robbery. High risk.
    • Success: Earn cash or items.
    • Fail: Pay a fine or go to jail (command lock).
  • /daily - Claim your free daily credits.

docs.title: 2. Passive Income (Investments)
docs.text:
• Business: Buy businesses (e.g., Lemonade Stand, Corporation) to earn hourly revenue.
  • Stock Market: (Coming Soon) Buy low, sell high.

docs.title: 3. Social Income
docs.text:
• /tips - Receive tips from other users.
  • /trade - Trade items/cash with users securely.

docs.title: Server Shops
docs.text:
Server Admins can create custom shops using /shop create. * User Command: /buy <item_name> * Admin Command: /shop add item:<name> price:<amount> role:<optional_reward_role>

Scenario: Buying a Role

A server owner creates a "VIP" role item for $50,000.

  1. User runs /buy item:VIP.
  2. $50,000 is deducted from their Global Wallet.
  3. The Bot automatically assigns the "VIP" role in that specific server.

docs.title: Global Market
docs.text: Buy rare collectibles that appear in the global rotation. * Command: /market view

docs.title: Gambling
docs.text:
Use responsibly.

  • /gamble slots <amount>
  • /gamble coinflip <amount> <heads/tails>
  • /gamble blackjack <amount>

docs.title: Leaderboards
docs.text:
Compete for the top spot.

  • /top rich - Global richest users.
  • /top local - Richest users in the current server.

docs.title: Moderation Module
docs.text: The Moderation module provides security, logging, and automated enforcement. It is designed to be "Set and Forget".

docs.title: Auto-Moderation (AutoMod)
docs.text:
Arachnid filters chat in real-time.

Command: /automod toggle <feature>

Feature Description Anti-Spam Prevents sending messages too quickly. Anti-Invite Deletes Discord invite links (ignores invites to the current server). Anti-Links Deletes all HTTP links (configurable whitelists). Caps-Lock Deletes messages with >70% capital letters.

docs.title: Verification Gate
docs.text:
Prevent raid bots from entering your server.

  1. Setup: /verification setup channel:#verify role:@Member
  2. Flow:
    • New user joins -> Sees only #verify.
    • User clicks "Verify" button.
    • Bot checks account age/flags.
    • Success: Bot gives @Member role.
    • Fail: Bot kicks user.

docs.title: Manual Actions
docs.text: All manual actions are logged to the configured log channel.

docs.title: Punishments
docs.text:
• /ban <user> [reason] [time]
    • Example: /ban @User "Raiding" 7d (Bans for 7 days).
  • /kick <user> [reason]
  • /mute <user> [time] [reason]
    • Uses Discord's native "Timeout" feature.
  • /warn <user> [reason]
    • Adds a strike to the user's record. 3 strikes = Kick (Configurable).

docs.title: Utilities
docs.text:
• /lock - Locks the current channel (denies @everyone Send Messages).
  • /unlock - Unlocks the channel.
  • /purge <amount> - Bulk deletes messages.
    • /purge user:@User 10 (Delete only 10 messages from specific user).
  • /slowmode <seconds> - Sets channel rate limit.

docs.title: Logging
docs.text:
Arachnid logs all actions to a dedicated channel.

Setup: 1. Create a channel (e.g., #mod-logs). 2. Run /settings logchannel #mod-logs.

What is logged? * Command usage (Mod actions). * Message edits/deletes. * Member join/leave. * Voice channel activity (Join/Move/Leave).

docs.title: Hi-Fi Music Module
docs.text: Arachnid's music module is designed for audiophiles. It supports high-fidelity streaming, seamless Spotify bridging, and advanced DSP (Digital Signal Processing) filters.

docs.title: Supported Sources
docs.text: Source Icon Support Type Quality YouTube Search, URL, Playlist 128-160kbps (Opus) SoundCloud Search, URL 128-256kbps Bandcamp URL High (Source dependent) Spotify URL (Bridge) High (Bridge dependent) Twitch Stream URL Live Audio

docs.title: Playback Control
docs.text:
User CommandsDJ Commands
  • /play <query> - Join VC and play a song.
  • /pause - Pause playback.
  • /resume - Resume playback.
  • /skip - Vote to skip the current song.
  • /queue - Show the upcoming songs.
  • /nowplaying - Show current song info + progress bar.

(Requires DJ role or Admin)

  • /stop - Stop playback and clear queue.
  • /force skip - Skip without voting.
  • /volume <0-100> - Change volume.
  • /seek <timestamp> - Jump to a specific time (e.g., 1:30).

docs.title: Audio Filters
docs.text:
Apply real-time effects to the audio stream.

Usage: /filter set <filter_name>

  • nightcore: Faster, higher pitch. Anime vibe.
  • vaporwave: Slower, lower pitch. Retro vibe.
  • bassboost: +10dB to low frequencies.
  • 8d: Rotates audio around the stereo field.
  • karaoke: Attempts to remove vocals (Mid-Side cancellation).

docs.title: The Spotify Bridge
docs.text:
When you paste a Spotify link, Arachnid does not stream from Spotify (this is technically impossible for bots).

  1. Bot reads track metadata: Artist - Title.
  2. Bot searches YouTube Music/SoundCloud for the best audio match.
  3. Bot streams the matched audio.

docs.title: Last.fm Integration
docs.text:
Track your listening habits.

  1. Create a Last.fm account.
  2. Run /fm login and authorize the bot.
  3. Scrobble: Every song you listen to is added to your Last.fm profile.
  4. Flex: Use /fm chart to generate a 3x3 grid of your top albums.

docs.title: AI Recommendations
docs.text:
If the queue is empty, enable Autoplay to have Arachnid guess what you want to hear next.

  • Command: /autoplay toggle
  • Logic: Uses your listening history + current song genre to queue similar tracks.

docs.title: Social & Leveling Module
docs.text: Build engagement with Arachnid's social features. Rewarding activity creates a stickier community.

docs.title: Leveling System
docs.text: Users earn XP (Experience Points) for chatting and being active in voice channels.

docs.title: Mechanics
docs.text:
• Chat XP: 15-25 XP per message (1 minute cooldown to prevent spam).
  • Voice XP: 10 XP per 5 minutes in a non-AFK voice channel.

docs.title: Rewards
docs.text:
Admins can set up Role Rewards that are automatically assigned when a user reaches a specific level.

Configuration: * /level config rewards add level:5 role:@Regular * /level config rewards add level:10 role:@Expert * /level config message <on/off> (Toggle "Level Up" messages in chat).

docs.title: Profiles
docs.text:
Every user has a customizable profile card.

Command: /profile

Displays: * Global Rank * Server Level & XP * Economy Balance * Reputation Points * Custom Bio (/profile setbio) * Custom Background (/profile setbg - Premium feature)

docs.title: Reputation
docs.text:
A community-driven trust system.

  • Give Rep: /rep @User
  • Cooldown: 24 Hours.
  • Purpose: Shows how trusted/helpful a user is on their profile.

docs.title: Sticky Messages
docs.text:
Pin a message to the bottom of a channel so it's always visible, even as new messages come in.

  • Command: /sticky set message:Read the rules!
  • Logic: When a new message is sent, the bot deletes the old sticky message and reposts it at the bottom.

docs.title: Command Reference
docs.text: A list of Arachnid's primary command categories. Use /help in Discord for the most up-to-date syntax.

docs.title: Server Management
docs.text:
• /wizard - Open the Server Architect interface to build channels and roles.
  • /help - View the help menu and command list.
  • /invite - Get the bot's invite link.

docs.title: Music
docs.text:
• /play - Play music from YouTube, SoundCloud, or Bandcamp.
  • /stop - Stop playback and disconnect.
  • /skip - Skip the current song.
  • /volume - Adjust playback volume.
  • /lyrics - Fetch lyrics for the current song.

docs.title: Economy
docs.text:
• /work - Earn currency by working.
  • /daily - Claim your daily reward.
  • /balance - Check your wallet and bank balance.
  • /shop - View or create server shops.
  • /gamble - Play games of chance (Slots, Blackjack).

docs.title: Moderation
docs.text:
• /ban - Ban a user from the server.
  • /kick - Kick a user.
  • /mute - Timeout a user.
  • /warn - Issue a warning to a user.
  • /purge - Bulk delete messages.

docs.title: Social
docs.text:
• /profile - View your custom profile card.
  • /rank - Check your level and XP.
  • /leaderboard - View the server or global leaderboard.
  • /rep - Give reputation points to another user.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text: Definitions of terms used within Arachnid's ecosystem.

docs.title: Cooldown
docs.text: The waiting period required before a command can be used again. * Example: /daily has a 24-hour cooldown.

docs.title: Embed
docs.text: A rich-media message box used by Discord bots to display information cleanly, often containing titles, descriptions, images, and color bars.

docs.title: Prefix
docs.text: The character(s) used to trigger a bot command. * Slash Command (/): The modern, standard way to use Arachnid. * Text Prefix (!): The legacy way (e.g., !play). Slash commands are recommended.

docs.title: Global Currency
docs.text: A currency system where your balance is stored on the bot's central database, not the specific server's database. Your money travels with you.

docs.title: Net Worth
docs.text: The total combined value of a user's Wallet + Bank + Inventory.

docs.title: RNG (Random Number Generator)
docs.text: Used in gambling and loot drops. Determines the outcome of chance-based events.

docs.title: Bridge
docs.text: The technology Arachnid uses to "play" Spotify links. Since Spotify does not allow direct bot streaming, the Bridge finds the equivalent audio on YouTube/SoundCloud.

docs.title: Scrobble
docs.text: The act of recording a track you listened to and sending that data to Last.fm for statistics.

docs.title: Blueprint
docs.text: The temporary design configuration created in the /wizard before the server is actually built.

docs.title: Category
docs.text: A folder in Discord that holds Text and Voice channels. Permissions set on a Category are usually inherited by the channels inside it.

docs.title: Template
docs.text: A saved configuration of a server's structure (Channels, Roles, perms) that can be loaded later or shared.
